title: How Dolphins Echolocate
script_summary: Explore how dolphins use echolocation to hunt and navigate underwater.

segments.voiceover: Dolphins click and read echoes, revealing secrets of the ocean.
Echolocating sounds travel through water, bouncing off objects.
With clicks reaching over a mile, they create a mental map.
Research shows these echoes help dolphins find prey in murky waters.
In a world of darkness, echolocation lights their path.
Curious about more marine wonders? Follow for deeper dives!
